docs.title: Qwibo
docs.text:
Qwibo is a Windows desktop app that transcribes audio and video to text offline, using the NVIDIA NeMo Parakeet TDT 0.6B v3 model. Optional LLM summaries run locally (Qwen) or via cloud APIs.

Bilingual documentation

This site is also available in Italiano. Use the language selector in the top navigation bar.

docs.title: What it does
docs.text: Feature Description Transcription Audio/video → plain text + SRT subtitles Job queue Multiple files processed one at a time; history and detail pages in the app Summary Multi-provider LLM (DeepSeek, OpenAI, local Qwen, …) Desktop app Electron installer — no Python, no terminal, no Docker Privacy Audio stays on your PC during transcription

docs.title: Who this is for
docs.text:
Qwibo is built for people who already have recordings — meetings, podcasts, interviews, lectures — and want usable text without uploading files to a cloud service or paying per minute.

For self-hosted Docker on a home server or NAS, see the separate qwibo-docker repository.

docs.title: Quick start
docs.text:
1. Download Qwibo-Setup-*.exe from GitHub Releases.
  2. Run the installer (see SmartScreen if Windows blocks it).
  3. Complete the first-run setup (~4–5 GB one-time model download).
  4. Upload a file and click Queue transcription.

Details: Download & install · First run

docs.title: Hardware at a glance
docs.text:
Resource Minimum Recommended Windows 10/11 64-bit 11 RAM (transcription only) 8 GB 16 GB RAM (local Qwen summary) 16 GB 32 GB Free disk 8 GB 12 GB GPU Not required NVIDIA optional (future)

Full details: System requirements

docs.title: Documentation map
docs.text: Section Content Getting started Install, first run, models User guide UI, queue, summaries, formats Troubleshooting Common errors Reference FAQ, licenses

docs.title: Build documentation locally
docs.text:
pip install -r docs/requirements.txt
mkdocs serve

Preview at http://127.0.0.1:8000 — publish with scripts\publish_docs.bat (or python scripts/publish_docs.py), then git push in the sibling repo qwibo.github.io. No GitHub Actions.

docs.title: License
docs.text: Proprietary software — Copyright © 2024-2026 Antonio Trento. Free for personal use; commercial use requires a paid license. See Licenses.

docs.title: 0.1.0-alpha.1
docs.text:
• First Windows desktop installer (Electron + embedded Python)
  • Offline transcription with NVIDIA NeMo Parakeet
  • Optional local Qwen summary (RAM ≥ 16 GB) or cloud LLM providers
  • Rebrand from Sbobinator to Qwibo
  • Documentation published at qwibo.github.io/docs

docs.title: Known limitations (alpha)
docs.text:
• Installer unsigned — SmartScreen warning
  • CPU-only ASR in desktop build
  • Model setup wizard (progress UI improvements planned)
  • Mac build not yet available

docs.title: Roadmap (high level)
docs.text:
• Code signing (Authenticode)
  • In-app model download progress
  • Mac DMG + notarization
  • UX polish (Phase 3 branding plan)

See evolutive/ in the repository for longer-term ideas.

docs.title: Build the Windows installer
docs.text: This section is for developers packaging Qwibo. End users only need the .exe from Releases.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text: Tool Version Windows 10/11 64-bit Python 3.12+ Node.js 20+

docs.title: Build commands
docs.text:
cd desktop-electron
build_installer.bat          REM first build (30–60 min)
build_installer.bat fast     REM incremental (~10–15 min)

Output: desktop-electron\release\Qwibo-Setup-*.exe

docs.title: What the build does
docs.text:
1. Syncs backend into backend/vendor/ (sync_backend.py)
  2. Downloads standalone Python + creates runtime-venv (torch, NeMo, FastAPI)
  3. Bundles static ffmpeg
  4. Packages Electron + NSIS installer

First build: 20–40 min (large PyTorch/NeMo download). Installer size: ~2–3 GB (normal for offline ASR).

docs.title: Dev mode (no installer)
docs.text:
After install.bat in desktop-electron/:

npm run dev

Uses local venv instead of embedded runtime.

docs.title: Runtime verification
docs.text:
cd desktop-electron
python scripts\verify_runtime.py

Checks llama-cpp baseline (no AVX) on target hardware.

docs.title: Independence from qwibo-docker
docs.text:
Desktop installer qwibo-docker Python Embedded Container ffmpeg Bundled In image User data %APPDATA%\qwibo-desktop\ Docker volume

See desktop-electron/README.md in the repo for release notes and troubleshooting.

docs.title: Download & install
docs.text: Qwibo ships as a single Windows installer. No Python, ffmpeg, or Docker setup required.

docs.title: Download
docs.text:
1. Open GitHub Releases for the qwibo repository.
  2. Download the latest Qwibo-Setup-*.exe (alpha builds are tagged 0.1.0-alpha.*).
  3. File size is roughly 2–3 GB — the installer bundles the runtime; models download on first run.

docs.title: Install
docs.text:
1. Double-click the installer.
  2. Accept the license and choose an install folder (default is fine).
  3. Finish the wizard — Qwibo opens automatically.

Windows SmartScreen

The alpha installer is not code-signed yet. Windows may show "Windows protected your PC". Click More info → Run anyway. See SmartScreen.

docs.title: After install
docs.text:
On first launch you will see the model setup wizard (~4–5 GB download, one time only):

Model Size Required Parakeet ASR ~2.5 GB Yes Qwen summary (local) ~2 GB Only if RAM ≥ 16 GB

When setup completes, the main window opens and you can queue your first file. See First run.

docs.title: Uninstall
docs.text:
Windows Settings → Apps → Qwibo → Uninstall.

User data in %APPDATA%\qwibo-desktop\ is not removed automatically. Delete that folder if you want a clean reinstall.

docs.title: Self-hosted Docker
docs.text: If you prefer a browser UI on a home server instead of the desktop app, use qwibo-docker — separate repo, separate docs (not published on this site).

docs.title: First run
docs.text: The first time you open Qwibo after installation, a setup wizard downloads the speech models. This happens once — not during your first transcription.

docs.title: Setup wizard
docs.text:
1. Qwibo starts and checks %APPDATA%\qwibo-desktop\models\.
  2. If Parakeet is missing, the wizard downloads it (~2.5 GB).
  3. If your PC has ≥ 16 GB RAM, Qwen for local summary is downloaded automatically (~2 GB).
  4. When finished, the main app window opens.

Be patient

Download time depends on your connection. The window may look idle — check tray logs if unsure (Open log folder).

docs.title: Main window
docs.text:
After setup:

  1. Home — upload area, sidebar with recent jobs, active queue.
  2. Queue & history — full list of all jobs.
  3. Summary settings — API keys and local engine status.
  4. License — terms and documentation links.

docs.title: First transcription (recommended test)
docs.text:
1. Pick a 10–20 minute file you know well (not a stress test on hour-long audio yet).
  2. Optional: enable Generate summary and pick an engine.
  3. Click Queue transcription.
  4. You are redirected to the job detail page with live progress.
  5. When complete: read trascrizione.txt, download SRT, open the output folder.

docs.title: Tray icon
docs.text:
Qwibo stays in the system tray when the window is closed (backend keeps running until you exit from the tray).

Tray action Effect Open Qwibo Restore main window Open log folder Diagnostic logs for support Quit Stop backend and exit

docs.title: Where files go
docs.text:
Job output lives under:

%APPDATA%\qwibo-desktop\data\output\jobs\YYYYMMDD_HHMMSS_filename\

See Jobs & output.

docs.title: If setup fails
docs.text:
Symptom What to try Download interrupted Restart Qwibo — setup resumes "Startup too slow" Antivirus blocking backend; check logs Out of disk space Free at least 8 GB on system drive

More: Common issues.

docs.title: Models
docs.text: Qwibo uses one required model for transcription and optional models for LLM summary. End users download these via the first-run wizard — no manual scripts.

docs.title: ASR model (required)
docs.text:
Property Value Hugging Face nvidia/parakeet-tdt-0.6b-v3 Local file %APPDATA%\qwibo-desktop\models\parakeet-tdt-0.6b-v3.nemo Size ~2.5 GB Engine NVIDIA NeMo

Optimized for Italian; other languages work with varying quality.

docs.title: Cloud LLM summary (optional)
docs.text:
No model on disk. Requires an API key for the chosen provider.

Provider Configure in app DeepSeek Summary settings OpenAI Summary settings Gemini Summary settings Claude Summary settings Kimi Summary settings

Keys are stored in %APPDATA%\qwibo-desktop\data\.secrets\summary_keys.json.

docs.title: Local LLM summary — Qwen (optional)
docs.text:
Property Value Model Qwen2.5-3B-Instruct (GGUF Q4) Path models\qwen2.5-3b-instruct\*.gguf Size ~2 GB RAM ≥ 16 GB system RAM Engine llama.cpp (CPU)

Downloaded automatically during first-run setup when RAM threshold is met.

docs.title: Summary table
docs.text: Component Location Download Network at runtime Parakeet ASR models\*.nemo Setup wizard No Cloud summary — API key only Yes Local Qwen models\qwen2.5-3b-instruct\ Setup wizard (if RAM OK) No

docs.title: Reinstall / clean slate
docs.text:
Uninstalling Qwibo does not delete models. To force re-download:

  1. Quit Qwibo from the tray.
  2. Delete %APPDATA%\qwibo-desktop\models\.
  3. Restart Qwibo — setup runs again.

docs.title: Licenses
docs.text:
• Parakeet: NVIDIA / NeMo terms
  • Qwen: Alibaba model license — see Hugging Face
  • Qwibo app: proprietary — see Licenses

Developers maintaining a dev build use scripts/download_model.py in the repo; that is not part of the retail installer flow.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: Qwibo turns audio and video into text you can search, edit, and reuse — on your own Windows PC.

docs.title: How it works
docs.text:
Installer (Electron)
    └── Embedded Python backend (FastAPI + worker)
            ├── Parakeet ASR  → trascrizione.txt + sottotitoli.srt
            └── Optional LLM  → riassunto.txt

You interact through a desktop window (not a browser tab you manage yourself). The app starts the backend automatically and keeps it running in the tray while Qwibo is open.

docs.title: Design principles
docs.text:
1. Local transcription — after the one-time model download, audio is not sent to the cloud for ASR.
  2. Offline models — Parakeet and optional Qwen GGUF live under your user profile.
  3. No overwrites — each job gets its own timestamped folder.
  4. One job at a time — the worker processes the queue sequentially to limit RAM use.

docs.title: Data location
docs.text:
All user data is under Electron userData (typically):

%APPDATA%\qwibo-desktop\
├── data\           ← jobs, input, output, secrets
├── models\         ← Parakeet .nemo + optional Qwen GGUF
├── logs\           ← diagnostic logs (tray → Open log folder)
└── cache\          ← internal caches (numba, matplotlib)

Open logs

Right-click the Qwibo tray icon → Open log folder if something fails during setup or transcription.

docs.title: Alpha status
docs.text: Current channel: 0.1.0-alpha — Windows installer, unsigned (SmartScreen warning). Mac and code signing are planned.

docs.title: Next steps
docs.text:
• Download & install
  • System requirements
  • First run
  • Models

docs.title: System requirements
docs.text: Requirements depend on what you use: transcription only, cloud API summary, or offline local summary (Qwen).

docs.title: Operating system
docs.text: Requirement Detail OS Windows 10 or 11 (64-bit) Admin Not required for normal use Internet Required for first-run model download and optional cloud summaries

docs.title: Comparison by scenario
docs.text:
Scenario RAM Free disk Network at runtime Transcription only 8 GB min · 16 GB recommended ~8 GB No (after setup) Transcription + API summary Same as above Same Yes during summary Transcription + local Qwen ≥ 16 GB · 32 GB ideal ~10–12 GB No (after setup)

RAM and local summary

Before local summary the pipeline unloads the ASR model from RAM. Qwibo checks total physical RAM; below 16 GB the local Qwen engine is not offered.

docs.title: Transcription only
docs.text: Resource Detail RAM Minimum 8 GB; 16 GB recommended for long files (1 h+). Disk ~2.5 GB ASR model + app data + job output → ≥ 8 GB free. CPU Modern x64; expect ~2× realtime on CPU (1 min audio ≈ 2 min processing). GPU Not used in the desktop alpha (CPU-only ASR).

docs.title: Transcription + cloud summary
docs.text:
Resource Detail RAM Same as transcription — LLM runs in the cloud. Disk No extra model for summary. Network Required when generating summaries. API key Configure in app → Summary settings.

Suitable for PCs with 8 GB RAM if you use cloud providers only.

docs.title: Transcription + local summary (Qwen)
docs.text:
Resource Detail RAM ≥ 16 GB (checked at setup). 32 GB recommended for long texts. Disk Transcription footprint + ~2 GB Qwen GGUF. CPU Summary is CPU-bound; long texts may take several minutes (map-reduce).

See also Models.

docs.title: Bundled dependencies (end user)
docs.text: The installer includes embedded Python, ffmpeg, and the Qwibo backend. You do not need to install these separately.

docs.title: Self-hosted alternative
docs.text: A Linux mini PC with more RAM/GPU can run qwibo-docker instead. Hardware guidance for Docker is documented in that repository.

docs.title: Commercial licensing
docs.text: Qwibo is proprietary software by Antonio Trento.

docs.title: Who needs a paid license?
docs.text:
A commercial license is required if you use Qwibo:

  • inside a company, public administration, or organization;
  • as a self-employed professional in the course of your work;
  • to provide paid or internal services to clients, staff, or users;
  • in any way beyond free personal / non-profit use.

Personal, educational, and documented non-profit use remains free.

docs.title: What you are licensing
docs.text:
You pay for permission to use the Qwibo application (desktop installer, updates, support channel as offered).

Open-source dependencies and model weights stay under their own licenses.

docs.title: How to buy
docs.text:
1. Contact https://antoniotrento.net with:
  2. organization name and country;
  3. number of seats or machines;
  4. use case (internal, client-facing, etc.).
  5. You receive a quote and commercial license agreement.
  6. After payment, confirmation for your compliance records.

License keys

In-app license keys for per-seat enforcement are planned for a later release. Early adopters may use written agreement + invoice.

docs.title: Distribution channels
docs.text:
Channel Status Desktop installer (Windows) Alpha — GitHub Releases Self-hosted Docker qwibo-docker Mac desktop Planned

Contact us before redistributing Qwibo inside your organization or to end customers.

docs.title: Does Qwibo send my audio over the internet?
docs.text: No for transcription. After the one-time model download, ASR runs locally. Cloud summaries send text (not audio) to your chosen provider if you enable them.

docs.title: Which languages are supported?
docs.text: Parakeet TDT 0.6B v3 is optimized for Italian. Other languages work with varying quality.

docs.title: Do I need Python or Docker?
docs.text: No. The desktop installer is self-contained. Docker is a separate product (qwibo-docker).

docs.title: Mac or Linux desktop?
docs.text: Not in alpha. Windows first; Mac DMG is planned.

docs.title: How much disk space?
docs.text:
Component Size Parakeet ASR ~2.5 GB Local Qwen ~2 GB App + jobs varies

Plan 8–12 GB free for a comfortable install.

docs.title: Re-download after update?
docs.text: Usually no — models stay in %APPDATA%\qwibo-desktop\models\. Release notes will say if a new model version is required.

docs.title: Why one file at a time?
docs.text: The worker runs FIFO to limit RAM (NeMo + optional Qwen are heavy).

docs.title: Same file twice?
docs.text: If the first job is completed, a new upload creates a new folder with a new timestamp.

docs.title: Close the window during a job?
docs.text: The worker continues until you Quit from the system tray.

docs.title: Browser vs desktop?
docs.text: Qwibo uses a local web UI inside Electron. You don't manage ports or bookmarks — the app handles it.

docs.title: Worker starts automatically?
docs.text: Yes. Each app launch starts the backend and worker subprocess.

docs.title: Cloud vs local?
docs.text: Mode Privacy Requirements Cloud API Text sent to provider API key + internet Local Qwen Fully offline ≥ 16 GB RAM + ~2 GB model

docs.title: Transcript OK but summary failed?
docs.text: Normal if API key missing or provider error. Text is in trascrizione.txt.

docs.title: How fast on CPU?
docs.text: Rough guide: ~2× realtime on a typical laptop i5 (1 min audio ≈ 2 min). Mini PCs may be slower.

docs.title: First job slower?
docs.text: Parakeet loads into RAM on first transcription (+1–2 min).

docs.title: How do I publish these docs?
docs.text:
From the qwibo repo root:

python scripts\publish_docs.py

or scripts\publish_docs.bat — builds MkDocs and copies HTML to ../qwibo.github.io/docs/, then commits there if git is initialized. No GitHub Actions. Push qwibo.github.io to publish.

docs.title: Licensing
docs.text: Personal use is free. Business use needs a commercial license.

docs.title: Licenses
docs.text: Qwibo is proprietary software © 2024–2026 Antonio Trento.

docs.title: Personal use
docs.text: Free for personal, educational, and documented non-profit use, subject to the terms in the LICENSE file shipped with the installer and repository.

docs.title: Commercial use
docs.text: Companies, professionals, and paid services require a commercial license.

docs.title: Third-party components
docs.text:
Qwibo bundles or depends on open-source software including:

Component License (typical) Python runtime PSF PyTorch BSD-style FastAPI MIT Electron MIT NVIDIA NeMo / Parakeet NVIDIA / model terms Qwen GGUF Alibaba model license

Each component remains under its own license. Your Qwibo license covers the Qwibo application, not relicensing of upstream projects.

docs.title: Model weights
docs.text:
• Parakeet: download subject to NVIDIA and Hugging Face terms
  • Qwen: see model card on Hugging Face

docs.title: Full text
docs.text: See LICENSE and LICENSE.it in the qwibo repository.

docs.title: Model download fails or stalls
docs.text:
Cause: Network drop, antivirus, or insufficient disk space.

Try:

  1. Tray → Open log folder — check backend log lines.
  2. Ensure ≥ 8 GB free on the system drive.
  3. Restart Qwibo — setup retries missing models.
  4. Temporarily allow Qwibo through antivirus.

docs.title: "Startup too slow" / app won't open
docs.text:
Cause: Backend did not respond within ~3 minutes (first load or blocked process).

Try:

  1. Quit from tray, wait 10 s, relaunch.
  2. Check no zombie python.exe from a previous crash (Task Manager).
  3. Read logs in %APPDATA%\qwibo-desktop\logs\.
  4. Reinstall if runtime-venv is corrupted.

docs.title: Qwibo opens but page is blank
docs.text:
Cause: Backend crash after window opened.

Try: Open log folder, restart app. If repeated, reinstall.

docs.title: Job stays running forever
docs.text:
Cause: Worker crash or very long file on slow CPU.

Try:

  1. Wait — first job includes model load (1–2 min extra).
  2. Check job detail for progress percentage.
  3. Restart Qwibo — orphaned jobs are recovered.
  4. Inspect logs for NeMo / ffmpeg errors.

docs.title: Job failed immediately
docs.text:
Cause: Corrupt file, unsupported codec, or missing ASR model.

Try:

  1. Open job detail — read error message.
  2. Confirm Parakeet exists in models\ (> 2.2 GB .nemo file).
  3. Test with a short .wav or .mp3.

docs.title: Poor Italian quality
docs.text:
Cause: Background noise, overlap, distant mic, strong accent.

Try: Shorter test clip in a quiet room. Parakeet is tuned for Italian but not magic.

docs.title: Summary not generated, transcript OK
docs.text:
Cause: Missing API key, local Qwen unavailable, or provider error.

Try:

  1. Summary settings — verify key or local engine status.
  2. For cloud: check internet and API quota.
  3. For local: RAM must be ≥ 16 GB; Qwen GGUF must exist in models\.

Transcript is always in trascrizione.txt.

docs.title: Local summary greyed out
docs.text:
Cause: RAM below 16 GB or Qwen not downloaded.

Use a cloud provider or transcription-only mode.

docs.title: Same file won't enqueue
docs.text:
Cause: An active job with the same filename is queued or running.

Try: Wait for completion, or use Enqueue anyway if shown.

docs.title: UI shows job but files missing
docs.text:
Cause: Manual folder deletion outside the app.

Try: Delete the stale job in UI, or Sync disk on /jobs.

docs.title: Very slow on mini PC
docs.text: Expected on CPU-only hardware (~2× realtime or worse). Queue long files overnight.

docs.title: Still stuck?
docs.text:
1. Tray → Open log folder
  2. Note Qwibo version (window title)
  3. Open an issue on GitHub with log excerpt (redact API keys)

SmartScreen during install: SmartScreen.

docs.title: Windows SmartScreen
docs.text: The Qwibo alpha installer is not yet signed with an Authenticode certificate. Windows Defender SmartScreen may block it.

docs.title: What you see
docs.text:
Windows protected your PC Microsoft Defender SmartScreen prevented an unrecognized app from starting.

This is expected for unsigned alpha builds.

docs.title: How to install anyway
docs.text:
1. Click More info (or Ulteriori informazioni in Italian Windows).
  2. Click Run anyway (Esegui comunque).

docs.title: Why unsigned?
docs.text: Code signing certificates cost money and require identity verification. Signing is on the roadmap for a stable release.

docs.title: Is the file safe?
docs.text:
Download only from official GitHub Releases on the qwibo/qwibo repository. Verify the filename matches Qwibo-Setup-*.exe.

After install, SmartScreen does not block normal app launches — only the installer triggers this warning.

docs.title: App interface
docs.text: The Qwibo desktop window embeds the same FastAPI + HTMX UI used in development, bound to 127.0.0.1 on a free local port (starting around 8765). You never type a URL — Electron opens it for you.

docs.title: Starting and quitting
docs.text:
Action How Start Launch from Start menu or desktop shortcut Minimize Close button → app stays in tray Quit Tray → Quit (stops backend worker)

Only one instance runs at a time. Opening Qwibo again focuses the existing window.

docs.title: Navigation
docs.text: Screen Path Description Home / Upload, live queue, recent jobs sidebar Queue & history /jobs Full table, filters, actions Job detail /jobs/{id} Transcript, summary, downloads, progress Summary settings /settings/summary API keys, engine status License /settings/license Terms, doc links

docs.title: Home (/)
docs.text:
• Drag-and-drop or file picker upload
  • Sidebar: Generate summary, engine, length
  • Active queue panel (updates every ~2 s)
  • Sidebar lists latest 8 jobs with links to detail pages

After enqueueing, you are redirected to /jobs/{id}.

docs.title: Queue & history (/jobs)
docs.text: Table of all jobs with search and filters. Click the file name or Open to view details.

docs.title: Job detail (/jobs/{id})
docs.text:
• In progress: progress bar with auto-refresh until complete
  • Completed: transcript, summary, download buttons (TXT / SRT / summary)
  • Open folder — opens the job directory in Explorer
  • Actions: cancel (queued only), retry, reprocess, delete

docs.title: Summary settings (/settings/summary)
docs.text:
• Enter cloud provider API keys
  • See which engines are available
  • Local Qwen status and RAM check

docs.title: Background worker
docs.text:
When the UI starts, a separate Python process runs the transcription worker (NeMo does not run inside the web server process).

If Qwibo crashes, orphaned running jobs are recovered on the next start.

docs.title: Tips
docs.text:
• First job after cold start: 1–2 minutes to load Parakeet into RAM
  • Same file twice → two folders with different timestamps — use /jobs to tell them apart
  • Logs: tray → Open log folder

See Jobs & output for queue logic.

docs.title: Audio
docs.text: .wav, .mp3, .flac, .m4a, .ogg, .opus, .aac, .wma

docs.title: Video
docs.text:
.mp4, .mkv, .avi, .mov, .webm, .m4v, .flv, .wmv

Audio is extracted with bundled ffmpeg at 16 kHz mono PCM (NeMo requirement).

docs.title: Output per job
docs.text: File Format Content trascrizione.txt UTF-8 plain text Full transcript sottotitoli.srt SubRip Timestamped segments riassunto.txt UTF-8 plain text Summary (if generated) source.* Original Copy of upload job.json JSON Job metadata

docs.title: SRT example
docs.text:
1
00:00:00,000 --> 00:00:05,120
First transcribed sentence.

2
00:00:05,120 --> 00:00:10,450
Second sentence.

docs.title: Long files
docs.text:
Threshold Behavior ≤ 30 minutes Single pass > 30 minutes 30 s chunks, 2 s overlap, merged

Long files take proportionally longer on CPU.

docs.title: Encoding
docs.text: All text output is UTF-8.

docs.title: Two data layers
docs.text:
Layer Location Role Registry data\output\jobs\queue.db Job list, states, timestamps Files data\output\jobs\{id}\ Audio copy, transcript, SRT, summary

Base path: %APPDATA%\qwibo-desktop\data\

The UI reads the list from SQLite and content from files. On startup the app syncs DB and disk.

docs.title: Job states
docs.text: State Meaning queued Waiting in line running Processing now completed Finished OK failed Error (see job detail / job.json) cancelled User cancelled while queued

docs.title: IDs and folders
docs.text:
Format: YYYYMMDD_HHMMSS_file-name

Example: 20260704_183045_interview-podcast

If two uploads share the same second: suffix _2, _3, …

docs.title: Folder contents
docs.text:
20260704_183045_interview-podcast/
├── source.mp3
├── job.json
├── trascrizione.txt
├── sottotitoli.srt
└── riassunto.txt        ← optional

docs.title: FIFO queue
docs.text:
1. Upload → job queued
  2. Worker claims next job → running
  3. Transcription + export + optional summary
  4. → completed or failed

One job at a time per worker instance.

docs.title: Duplicates
docs.text:
Situation Behavior Same filename queued/running Skipped by default Same filename already completed New job, new folder Re-upload with "Enqueue anyway" Forces duplicate in queue

No overwrite of past jobs.

docs.title: Actions (UI)
docs.text: Action When Effect Cancel queued → cancelled Retry failed / cancelled Same job requeued Reprocess completed New run, transcript only + Summary completed Reprocess with summary Delete Not running Removes DB row + folder Sync disk Anytime Reconcile DB ↔ filesystem

docs.title: After restart
docs.text:
• queue.db persists — history remains
  • Worker resumes queued jobs
  • Stale running jobs recovered on startup

docs.title: Manual cleanup
docs.text:
Prefer Delete in the UI. To wipe everything:

  1. Quit Qwibo from tray.
  2. Delete %APPDATA%\qwibo-desktop\data\output\jobs\.

Models in models\ are not affected.

docs.title: Text summarization
docs.text: After transcription, Qwibo can generate an LLM summary from trascrizione.txt.

docs.title: Providers
docs.text:
ID Engine Setup deepseek DeepSeek API API key in Summary settings openai OpenAI API key gemini Google Gemini API key claude Anthropic Claude API key kimi Moonshot Kimi API key local Qwen2.5 GGUF (CPU) First-run download + RAM ≥ 16 GB

Configure keys in the app: Summary settings (/settings/summary).

Keys saved to: %APPDATA%\qwibo-desktop\data\.secrets\summary_keys.json

docs.title: Length
docs.text: Value Behavior auto Proportional to text length short Brief normal Balanced detailed Longer

docs.title: Pipeline
docs.text:
1. NeMo transcription completes
  2. ASR model unloaded from RAM
  3. summarize() — chosen provider; map-reduce for long texts
  4. Saves riassunto.txt

If summary fails: job stays completed, transcript and SRT remain valid; error shown on job page.

docs.title: Long texts
docs.text:
Beyond provider context → map-reduce (chunk summaries merged).

Local Qwen on CPU can take several minutes for hour-long transcripts.

docs.title: UI workflow
docs.text:
1. Summary settings — add API key (first time for cloud)
  2. Home sidebar → Generate summary on
  3. Pick engine + length
  4. Queue file

docs.title: Privacy note
docs.text:
Step Data leaves PC? Transcription No Cloud summary Yes — text only, to your provider Local Qwen No

Choose local or skip summary if transcripts are highly sensitive.

docs.title: Transcribe & summarize
docs.text: Short workflow for your first real file.

docs.title: 1. Open Qwibo
docs.text: Ensure first-run model setup is complete (First run).

docs.title: 2. Optional — summary settings
docs.text:
If you want a summary:

  1. Go to Summary settings (/settings/summary).
  2. Paste an API key (e.g. DeepSeek), or confirm local Qwen is available.
  3. Return to Home.

docs.title: 3. Configure sidebar
docs.text:
On the home page sidebar:

  • Generate summary — on/off
  • Engine — deepseek, openai, local, …
  • Length — auto, short, normal, detailed

docs.title: 4. Upload and queue
docs.text:
1. Choose your audio or video file.
  2. Click Queue transcription.
  3. Wait on the job detail page — progress updates automatically.

docs.title: 5. Use the results
docs.text:
Output Use Transcript Copy from UI or download TXT Subtitles Download SRT for editors / YouTube Summary Download or read in UI

Open folder shows files on disk:

trascrizione.txt
sottotitoli.srt
riassunto.txt      ← if summary succeeded
source.*           ← copy of upload
job.json           ← metadata

docs.title: 6. Queue another file
docs.text: Return to Home or stay on /jobs — the worker processes one file at a time in FIFO order.

docs.title: Common first-time mistakes
docs.text:
Mistake Fix Closing the window during a job OK — worker continues until you Quit from tray Expecting instant results on long files Normal on CPU — check progress on job page Summary failed but transcript OK Read summary_error on job page; transcript is still valid

Details: Summarization · File formats

docs.title: Qwibo
docs.text:
Qwibo è un'app desktop per Windows che trascrive audio e video in testo offline, con il modello NVIDIA NeMo Parakeet TDT 0.6B v3. I riassunti LLM opzionali possono essere locali (Qwen) o via API cloud.

Documentazione bilingue

Questa documentazione è disponibile anche in inglese. Usa il selettore lingua in alto.

docs.title: Cosa fa
docs.text: Funzione Descrizione Trascrizione Audio/video → testo + sottotitoli SRT Coda job Più file elaborati uno alla volta; storico e dettaglio in app Riassunto LLM multi-provider (DeepSeek, OpenAI, Qwen locale, …) App desktop Installer Electron — niente Python, terminale o Docker Privacy L'audio resta sul PC durante la trascrizione

docs.title: A chi serve
docs.text:
Qwibo è per chi ha già registrazioni — riunioni, podcast, interviste, lezioni — e vuole testo utilizzabile senza caricare file su cloud né pagare al minuto.

Per Docker self-hosted su mini PC o NAS, vedi il repo qwibo-docker.

docs.title: Avvio rapido
docs.text:
1. Scarica Qwibo-Setup-*.exe dalle GitHub Releases.
  2. Esegui l'installer (vedi SmartScreen se Windows blocca).
  3. Completa il setup iniziale (~4–5 GB di modelli, una sola volta).
  4. Carica un file e clicca Accoda trascrizione.

Dettagli: Download · Primo avvio

docs.title: Hardware in sintesi
docs.text:
Risorsa Minimo Consigliato Windows 10/11 64-bit 11 RAM (solo ASR) 8 GB 16 GB RAM (Qwen locale) 16 GB 32 GB Disco libero 8 GB 12 GB GPU Non richiesta NVIDIA (futuro)

Dettagli: Requisiti di sistema

docs.title: Mappa documentazione
docs.text: Sezione Contenuto Per iniziare Installazione, primo avvio, modelli Guida utente Interfaccia, coda, riassunti Risoluzione problemi Errori comuni Riferimento FAQ, licenze

docs.title: Anteprima e pubblicazione docs
docs.text:
pip install -r docs/requirements.txt
mkdocs serve

Anteprima su http://127.0.0.1:8000 — pubblicazione: scripts\publish_docs.bat (o python scripts/publish_docs.py), poi git push nel repo gemello qwibo.github.io. Nessuna GitHub Action.

docs.title: Licenza
docs.text: Software proprietario — Copyright © 2024-2026 Antonio Trento. Uso personale gratuito; uso commerciale con licenza a pagamento. Vedi Licenze.

docs.title: 0.1.0-alpha.1
docs.text:
• Primo installer desktop Windows (Electron + Python embedded)
  • Trascrizione offline con NVIDIA NeMo Parakeet
  • Riassunto locale Qwen (RAM ≥ 16 GB) o provider cloud
  • Rebrand da Sbobinator a Qwibo
  • Documentazione su qwibo.github.io/docs

docs.title: Limiti noti (alpha)
docs.text:
• Installer non firmato — avviso SmartScreen
  • ASR solo CPU nel build desktop
  • Wizard modelli (miglioramenti UI progress bar in roadmap)
  • Mac non ancora disponibile

docs.title: Roadmap
docs.text:
• Firma codice (Authenticode)
  • Progress bar download modelli in-app
  • Mac DMG + notarization
  • UX (fase 3 piano branding)

Vedi cartella evolutive/ nel repository per idee a lungo termine.

docs.title: Creare l'installer Windows
docs.text: Sezione per sviluppatori. L'utente finale usa solo l'.exe dalle Release.

docs.title: Prerequisiti
docs.text: Windows 10/11, Python 3.12+, Node.js 20+.

docs.title: Comandi
docs.text:
cd desktop-electron
build_installer.bat          REM prima build (30–60 min)
build_installer.bat fast     REM incrementale (~10–15 min)

Output: desktop-electron\release\Qwibo-Setup-*.exe

docs.title: Cosa fa la build
docs.text:
1. Sync backend in backend/vendor/
  2. Python standalone + runtime-venv (torch, NeMo, FastAPI)
  3. ffmpeg statico
  4. Electron + NSIS

Prima build: 20–40 min. Installer: ~2–3 GB.

docs.title: Dev rapido
docs.text:
Dopo install.bat:

npm run dev

docs.title: Verifica runtime
docs.text:
python scripts\verify_runtime.py

Controlla baseline llama-cpp (no AVX).

Vedi desktop-electron/README.md nel repo.

docs.title: Download e installazione
docs.text: Qwibo si installa con un unico file .exe. Niente Python, ffmpeg o Docker.

docs.title: Download
docs.text:
1. Apri le GitHub Releases del repo qwibo.
  2. Scarica l'ultimo Qwibo-Setup-*.exe (alpha: tag 0.1.0-alpha.*).
  3. Dimensione ~2–3 GB — il runtime è nell'installer; i modelli si scaricano al primo avvio.

docs.title: Installazione
docs.text:
1. Doppio click sull'installer.
  2. Accetta la licenza e conferma la cartella (default ok).
  3. Fine wizard — Qwibo si apre da solo.

Windows SmartScreen

L'installer alpha non è ancora firmato. Windows può mostrare "Windows ha protetto il PC". Ulteriori informazioni → Esegui comunque. Vedi SmartScreen.

docs.title: Dopo l'installazione
docs.text:
Al primo avvio parte il wizard setup modelli (~4–5 GB, una volta):

Modello Dimensione Obbligatorio Parakeet ASR ~2,5 GB Sì Qwen locale ~2 GB Solo se RAM ≥ 16 GB

Al termine si apre la finestra principale. Vedi Primo avvio.

docs.title: Disinstallazione
docs.text:
Impostazioni → App → Qwibo → Disinstalla.

I dati in %APPDATA%\qwibo-desktop\ non vengono rimossi automaticamente. Elimina quella cartella per reinstallazione pulita.

docs.title: Docker self-hosted
docs.text: Per un'interfaccia browser su server di casa: qwibo-docker — repo e documentazione separati.

docs.title: Primo avvio
docs.text: La prima volta che apri Qwibo dopo l'installazione, un wizard scarica i modelli vocali. Succede una volta — non durante la prima trascrizione.

docs.title: Wizard setup
docs.text:
1. Qwibo controlla %APPDATA%\qwibo-desktop\models\.
  2. Se manca Parakeet, lo scarica (~2,5 GB).
  3. Con ≥ 16 GB RAM, scarica anche Qwen per riassunto locale (~2 GB).
  4. Si apre la finestra principale.

Pazienza

Il tempo dipende dalla connessione. Se sembra fermo, controlla i log dalla tray (Apri cartella log).

docs.title: Finestra principale
docs.text:
Dopo il setup:

  1. Home — upload, coda attiva, job recenti.
  2. Coda e storico — elenco completo.
  3. Impostazioni riassunto — API key e motori.
  4. Licenza — termini e link documentazione.

docs.title: Prima trascrizione (test consigliato)
docs.text:
1. File da 10–20 minuti che conosci bene.
  2. Opzionale: Genera riassunto + motore.
  3. Accoda trascrizione.
  4. Pagina dettaglio job con progresso live.
  5. A fine job: leggi trascrizione, scarica SRT, apri cartella output.

docs.title: Icona tray
docs.text:
Qwibo resta nella tray se chiudi la finestra (backend attivo finché non esci dalla tray).

Azione Effetto Apri Qwibo Ripristina finestra Apri cartella log Log diagnostici Esci Ferma backend ed esce

docs.title: Dove finiscono i file
docs.text:
%APPDATA%\qwibo-desktop\data\output\jobs\YYYYMMDD_HHMMSS_nomefile\

Vedi Coda e output.

docs.title: Se il setup fallisce
docs.text:
Sintomo Cosa provare Download interrotto Riavvia Qwibo "Avvio troppo lento" Antivirus; controlla log Disco pieno Libera almeno 8 GB

Altro: Problemi comuni.

docs.title: Modelli
docs.text: Qwibo usa un modello obbligatorio per la trascrizione e modelli opzionali per il riassunto. L'utente li scarica con il wizard — niente script manuali.

docs.title: Modello ASR (obbligatorio)
docs.text:
Proprietà Valore Hugging Face nvidia/parakeet-tdt-0.6b-v3 File locale %APPDATA%\qwibo-desktop\models\parakeet-tdt-0.6b-v3.nemo Dimensione ~2,5 GB Motore NVIDIA NeMo

Ottimizzato per italiano; altre lingue con qualità variabile.

docs.title: Riassunto cloud (opzionale)
docs.text:
Nessun modello su disco. Serve API key del provider.

Provider Configurazione DeepSeek, OpenAI, Gemini, Claude, Kimi Impostazioni riassunto

Chiavi in %APPDATA%\qwibo-desktop\data\.secrets\summary_keys.json.

docs.title: Riassunto locale — Qwen (opzionale)
docs.text:
Proprietà Valore Modello Qwen2.5-3B-Instruct (GGUF Q4) Percorso models\qwen2.5-3b-instruct\*.gguf Dimensione ~2 GB RAM ≥ 16 GB Motore llama.cpp (CPU)

Scaricato automaticamente al setup se la RAM lo consente.

docs.title: Tabella riepilogo
docs.text: Componente Posizione Download Rete in uso Parakeet models\*.nemo Wizard No Cloud — Solo API key Sì Qwen locale models\qwen2.5-3b-instruct\ Wizard No

docs.title: Reinstallazione pulita
docs.text:
1. Esci dalla tray.
  2. Elimina %APPDATA%\qwibo-desktop\models\.
  3. Riavvia Qwibo — il wizard riparte.

docs.title: Licenze modelli
docs.text:
• Parakeet: termini NVIDIA / NeMo
  • Qwen: licenza Alibaba — vedi Hugging Face
  • App Qwibo: proprietaria — Licenze

Gli sviluppatori usano scripts/download_model.py nel repo; non fa parte del flusso retail.

docs.title: Panoramica
docs.text: Qwibo trasforma audio e video in testo che puoi cercare, modificare e riusare — sul tuo PC Windows.

docs.title: Come funziona
docs.text:
Installer (Electron)
    └── Backend Python embedded (FastAPI + worker)
            ├── Parakeet ASR  → trascrizione.txt + sottotitoli.srt
            └── LLM opzionale → riassunto.txt

Interagisci con una finestra desktop (non un tab del browser da gestire). L'app avvia il backend e lo tiene in esecuzione nella tray finché Qwibo è aperto.

docs.title: Principi
docs.text:
1. Trascrizione locale — dopo il download iniziale, l'audio non va in cloud per l'ASR.
  2. Modelli offline — Parakeet e Qwen GGUF opzionale nel profilo utente.
  3. Nessun overwrite — ogni job ha la sua cartella con timestamp.
  4. Un job alla volta — coda sequenziale per limitare la RAM.

docs.title: Dove sono i dati
docs.text:
%APPDATA%\qwibo-desktop\
├── data\           ← job, input, output, secrets
├── models\         ← Parakeet .nemo + Qwen opzionale
├── logs\           ← log diagnostici (tray → Apri cartella log)
└── cache\          ← cache interne

Log

Tray Qwibo → Apri cartella log se qualcosa fallisce in setup o trascrizione.

docs.title: Stato alpha
docs.text: Canale attuale: 0.1.0-alpha — installer Windows non firmato (avviso SmartScreen). Mac e firma codice in roadmap.

docs.title: Prossimi passi
docs.text:
• Download e installazione
  • Requisiti di sistema
  • Primo avvio
  • Modelli

docs.title: Requisiti di sistema
docs.text: I requisiti dipendono da cosa usi: solo trascrizione, riassunto API cloud o riassunto locale Qwen.

docs.title: Sistema operativo
docs.text: Requisito Dettaglio OS Windows 10 o 11 (64-bit) Admin Non richiesto per uso normale Internet Per download modelli e riassunti cloud opzionali

docs.title: Confronto scenari
docs.text:
Scenario RAM Disco libero Rete in uso Solo trascrizione 8 GB min · 16 GB consigliati ~8 GB No (dopo setup) Trascrizione + API Come sopra Come sopra Sì per riassunto Trascrizione + Qwen locale ≥ 16 GB · 32 GB ideali ~10–12 GB No (dopo setup)

RAM e Qwen

Prima del riassunto locale il modello ASR viene scaricato dalla RAM. Sotto 16 GB Qwen non viene offerto.

docs.title: Solo trascrizione
docs.text: Risorsa Dettaglio RAM Minimo 8 GB; 16 GB per file lunghi (1 h+). Disco ~2,5 GB modello + dati job → ≥ 8 GB liberi. CPU x64 moderna; ~2× tempo reale (1 min audio ≈ 2 min). GPU Non usata nell'alpha desktop (ASR su CPU).

docs.title: Trascrizione + riassunto cloud
docs.text:
Risorsa Dettaglio RAM Come solo trascrizione Rete Richiesta durante il riassunto API key In app → Impostazioni riassunto

Adatto a PC con 8 GB RAM se usi solo provider cloud.

docs.title: Trascrizione + Qwen locale
docs.text:
Risorsa Dettaglio RAM ≥ 16 GB (controllo al setup) Disco + ~2 GB GGUF CPU Riassunto CPU-bound; testi lunghi = diversi minuti

Vedi Modelli.

docs.title: Dipendenze incluse
docs.text: L'installer contiene Python embedded, ffmpeg e backend Qwibo. L'utente finale non installa nulla a mano.

docs.title: Alternativa self-hosted
docs.text: Mini PC Linux con più RAM/GPU: qwibo-docker.

docs.title: Licenza commerciale
docs.text: Qwibo è software proprietario di Antonio Trento.

docs.title: Chi deve pagare?
docs.text:
Licenza commerciale se usi Qwibo:

  • in azienda, PA o organizzazione;
  • come professionista nell'attività lavorativa;
  • per servizi a pagamento o interni a clienti/utenti;
  • oltre l'uso personale gratuito.

docs.title: Cosa acquisti
docs.text:
Permesso d'uso dell'applicazione Qwibo (installer, aggiornamenti, supporto come offerto).

Dipendenze open source e pesi modelli restano sotto le rispettive licenze.

docs.title: Come acquistare
docs.text:
1. Contatta https://antoniotrento.net con organizzazione, paese, postazioni, caso d'uso.
  2. Preventivo e contratto.
  3. Conferma post-pagamento.

Chiavi licenza

Chiavi in-app per postazione in roadmap. Early adopter: accordo scritto + fattura.

docs.title: Canali
docs.text:
Canale Stato Desktop Windows Alpha — GitHub Releases Docker qwibo-docker Mac Pianificato

Contattaci prima di ridistribuire Qwibo in organizzazione o a clienti finali.

docs.title: Qwibo invia l'audio su internet?
docs.text: No per la trascrizione. I riassunti cloud inviano solo testo al provider scelto.

docs.title: Lingue supportate?
docs.text: Parakeet ottimizzato per italiano; altre lingue con qualità variabile.

docs.title: Serve Python o Docker?
docs.text: No per l'app desktop. Docker è qwibo-docker.

docs.title: Mac o Linux desktop?
docs.text: Non in alpha. Windows prima; Mac in roadmap.

docs.title: Spazio disco?
docs.text: Parakeet ~2,5 GB + Qwen ~2 GB + dati job. Pianifica 8–12 GB liberi.

docs.title: Riscaricare dopo aggiornamento?
docs.text: Di solito no — modelli in %APPDATA%\qwibo-desktop\models\.

docs.title: Perché un file alla volta?
docs.text: Coda FIFO per limitare RAM.

docs.title: Stesso file due volte?
docs.text: Se il primo job è completed → nuova cartella con nuovo timestamp.

docs.title: Chiudo la finestra?
docs.text: Il worker continua finché non Esci dalla tray.

docs.title: Riassunto
docs.text: Modalità Privacy Requisiti Cloud Testo al provider API key + rete Qwen locale Offline ≥ 16 GB RAM

docs.title: Come pubblico le docs?
docs.text:
Dal repo qwibo:

python scripts\publish_docs.py

Build MkDocs → copia in ../qwibo.github.io/docs/, commit opzionale. Nessuna GitHub Action. Push su qwibo.github.io.

docs.title: Licenza
docs.text: Uso personale gratuito. Uso commerciale: licenza commerciale.

docs.title: Licenze
docs.text: Qwibo è software proprietario © 2024–2026 Antonio Trento.

docs.title: Uso personale
docs.text: Gratuito per uso personale, didattico e non-profit documentato, secondo LICENSE nell'installer e nel repository.

docs.title: Uso commerciale
docs.text: Richiede licenza commerciale.

docs.title: Componenti third-party
docs.text: Python, PyTorch, FastAPI, Electron, NeMo/Parakeet, Qwen GGUF — ciascuno con la propria licenza. La licenza Qwibo copre l'applicazione, non la relicenza degli upstream.

docs.title: Testo completo
docs.text: LICENSE e LICENSE.it nel repository qwibo.

docs.title: Download modelli fallito o bloccato
docs.text: Prova: log dalla tray, ≥ 8 GB disco libero, riavvia Qwibo, controlla antivirus.

docs.title: "Avvio troppo lento"
docs.text:
Backend non risponde in ~3 minuti.

Prova: esci da tray, attendi, riapri; chiudi python.exe zombie; leggi log; reinstalla se necessario.

docs.title: Finestra bianca
docs.text: Backend crashato dopo apertura. Log + riavvio.

docs.title: Job running a lungo
docs.text: Attendi (primo job + model load). Controlla progresso. Riavvia se bloccato.

docs.title: Job failed
docs.text: File corrotto, codec non supportato, modello ASR mancante. Prova clip corta .wav/.mp3.

docs.title: Qualità italiana scarsa
docs.text: Rumore, sovrapposizioni, microfono lontano. Prova campione in ambiente quieto.

docs.title: Trascrizione OK, riassunto no
docs.text: Chiave API, rete, Qwen assente o RAM insufficiente. Trascrizione sempre in trascrizione.txt.

docs.title: Stesso file non si accoda
docs.text: Job attivo con stesso nome. Attendi o Accoda comunque.

docs.title: Performance
docs.text: Mini PC lenti: ~2× tempo reale o peggio. Accoda file lunghi di notte.

docs.title: Ancora bloccato?
docs.text:
Tray → Apri cartella log → issue su GitHub (senza chiavi API).

SmartScreen: SmartScreen.

docs.title: Windows SmartScreen
docs.text: L'installer alpha Qwibo non è ancora firmato con certificato Authenticode. SmartScreen può bloccarlo.

docs.title: Messaggio tipico
docs.text:
Windows ha protetto il PC Microsoft Defender SmartScreen ha impedito l'avvio di un'app non riconosciuta.

Normale per build alpha non firmate.

docs.title: Come installare
docs.text:
1. Ulteriori informazioni
  2. Esegui comunque

docs.title: Perché non firmato?
docs.text: I certificati costano e richiedono verifica identità. La firma è in roadmap.

docs.title: Sicurezza
docs.text:
Scarica solo dalle GitHub Releases ufficiali (Qwibo-Setup-*.exe).

Dopo l'installazione SmartScreen non blocca l'app — solo l'installer.

docs.title: Interfaccia
docs.text: La finestra Qwibo incorpora l'interfaccia FastAPI + HTMX su 127.0.0.1 e una porta locale libera (da ~8765). Non devi digitare URL — Electron la apre per te.

docs.title: Avvio e chiusura
docs.text:
Azione Come Avvia Menu Start o collegamento desktop Minimizza Chiudi finestra → resta in tray Esci Tray → Esci (ferma worker)

Una sola istanza: riaprire Qwibo mette a fuoco la finestra esistente.

docs.title: Navigazione
docs.text: Schermata Percorso Descrizione Home / Upload, coda, job recenti Coda e storico /jobs Tabella completa Dettaglio job /jobs/{id} Trascrizione, riassunto, download Impostazioni riassunto /settings/summary API key, motori Licenza /settings/license Termini, link docs

docs.title: Home
docs.text:
• Upload file
  • Sidebar: Genera riassunto, motore, lunghezza
  • Coda attiva (aggiornamento ~2 s)
  • Ultimi 8 job nella sidebar

Dopo l'accodamento → redirect a /jobs/{id}.

docs.title: Dettaglio job
docs.text:
• In corso: barra progresso con refresh automatico
  • Completato: trascrizione, riassunto, download TXT/SRT
  • Apri cartella → Explorer
  • Azioni: annulla, riprova, rielabora, elimina

docs.title: Impostazioni riassunto
docs.text:
• Chiavi API cloud
  • Stato motori disponibili
  • Controllo RAM per Qwen locale

docs.title: Worker in background
docs.text:
Processo Python separato per la trascrizione (NeMo non gira nel processo web).

Dopo un crash, i job running orfani vengono recuperati al riavvio.

docs.title: Suggerimenti
docs.text:
• Primo job a freddo: 1–2 min extra per caricare Parakeet
  • Stesso file due volte → due cartelle con timestamp diversi
  • Log: tray → Apri cartella log

Vedi Coda e output.

docs.title: Input supportati
docs.text:
Audio: .wav, .mp3, .flac, .m4a, .ogg, .opus, .aac, .wma

Video: .mp4, .mkv, .avi, .mov, .webm, .m4v, .flv, .wmv

Estrazione audio con ffmpeg incluso (16 kHz mono PCM).

docs.title: Output per job
docs.text: File Contenuto trascrizione.txt Testo completo UTF-8 sottotitoli.srt SubRip con timestamp riassunto.txt Riassunto (se generato) source.* Copia upload job.json Metadati

docs.title: File lunghi
docs.text:
Soglia Comportamento ≤ 30 min Passaggio unico > 30 min Chunk 30 s, overlap 2 s

Su CPU i tempi crescono proporzionalmente.

docs.title: Codifica
docs.text: Tutto in UTF-8.

docs.title: Due livelli dati
docs.text:
Livello Percorso Ruolo Registro data\output\jobs\queue.db Elenco job, stati File data\output\jobs\{id}\ Audio, trascrizione, SRT

Base: %APPDATA%\qwibo-desktop\data\

docs.title: Stati job
docs.text: Stato Significato queued In attesa running In elaborazione completed OK failed Errore cancelled Annullato in coda

docs.title: ID e cartelle
docs.text:
Formato: YYYYMMDD_HHMMSS_nome-file

Collisione nello stesso secondo: suffisso _2, _3, …

docs.title: Contenuto cartella
docs.text:
20260704_183045_intervista/
├── source.mp3
├── job.json
├── trascrizione.txt
├── sottotitoli.srt
└── riassunto.txt

docs.title: Coda FIFO
docs.text: Un job alla volta. Nessun overwrite dei job passati.

docs.title: Duplicati
docs.text: Situazione Comportamento Stesso nome in coda/running Saltato (default) Stesso nome già completato Nuovo job, nuova cartella "Accoda comunque" Forza duplicato

docs.title: Azioni UI
docs.text: Annulla (solo queued), riprova, rielabora, + riassunto, elimina, sincronizza disco.

docs.title: Pulizia
docs.text: Preferisci Elimina in UI. Per azzerare tutto: esci da tray e cancella data\output\jobs\. I modelli in models\ restano.

docs.title: Riassunto testuale
docs.text: Dopo la trascrizione, Qwibo può generare un riassunto LLM da trascrizione.txt.

docs.title: Provider
docs.text:
ID Motore Setup deepseek API DeepSeek Chiave in impostazioni openai OpenAI Chiave gemini Google Gemini Chiave claude Anthropic Chiave kimi Moonshot Chiave local Qwen GGUF Setup + RAM ≥ 16 GB

Chiavi in %APPDATA%\qwibo-desktop\data\.secrets\summary_keys.json.

docs.title: Lunghezza
docs.text: auto, short, normal, detailed.

docs.title: Pipeline
docs.text:
1. Trascrizione NeMo
  2. Scarico ASR dalla RAM
  3. Riassunto (map-reduce se testo lungo)
  4. Salva riassunto.txt

Se fallisce: job completed, trascrizione e SRT validi.

docs.title: Privacy
docs.text:
Passo Esce dal PC? Trascrizione No Riassunto cloud Sì — solo testo Qwen locale No

Per dati sensibili: local o disabilita riassunto.

docs.title: Trascrivere e riassumere
docs.text: Workflow rapido per il primo file reale.

docs.title: 1. Apri Qwibo
docs.text: Setup modelli completato (Primo avvio).

docs.title: 2. Opzionale — impostazioni riassunto
docs.text:
1. Impostazioni riassunto (/settings/summary).
  2. Incolla API key (es. DeepSeek) oppure verifica Qwen locale.
  3. Torna alla Home.

docs.title: 3. Sidebar
docs.text:
• Genera riassunto — on/off
  • Motore — deepseek, openai, local, …
  • Lunghezza — auto, short, normal, detailed

docs.title: 4. Carica e accoda
docs.text:
1. Scegli audio o video.
  2. Accoda trascrizione.
  3. Attendi sulla pagina dettaglio job.

docs.title: 5. Risultati
docs.text:
Output Uso Trascrizione Copia da UI o TXT Sottotitoli SRT per editor / YouTube Riassunto Download o lettura in UI

Apri cartella:

trascrizione.txt
sottotitoli.srt
riassunto.txt
source.*
job.json

docs.title: 6. Accoda altro
docs.text: Torna alla Home o usa /jobs — un file alla volta in ordine FIFO.

docs.title: Errori comuni
docs.text:
Errore Fix Chiudi finestra durante job OK — worker attivo finché non Esci da tray File lungo lento Normale su CPU Riassunto fallito, trascrizione OK Leggi errore in pagina job

Dettagli: Riassunto · Formati
